name: agent-sort
tagline: Sorts an ECC bundle's skills, commands, rules, and hooks into DAILY vs LIBRARY buckets for a specific repo using grep-based evidence.
verdict: Evidence-driven classification workflow with a clearly defined output format and disclosed optional side effect (creating a library router skill). Gaps: no guidance on when NOT to use it, no handling for missing tools/files during the repo scan (rg absent, package.json missing), rg is invoked without being declared or checked, and there is no.

highlights.summary: No 'NOT for' / out-of-scope / pointer-to-other-skill guidance about when this skill itself should not be used.
Multi-step flow runs rg, cat package.json/pyproject.toml/etc. with no mention of what to do if a tool is missing or a file doesn't exist.
Uses `rg` (ripgrep) throughout the evidence-gathering commands with no requirements note or availability check.
